name: FFIST
report: The _airdrop() function is intended to distribute $FFIST tokens randomly to different addresses. Unfortunately, the randomness of airdrop addresses can be predicted due to manipulable parameters such as the last airdrop address, block number, from & to addresses. Moreover, the number of tokens airdropped to an address is fixed at one token, leading to an imbalance in the pool and causing $FFIST to be consistently set to 1. This imbalance allows a small amount of $FFIST tokens to be exchanged for a disproportionately large amount of USD. $FFIST suffered an attack due to a business logic issue leading to a loss of ～110K USD.
